Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient registered on the DigiLab Connect platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the patient.

lastName.description: The last name of the patient.

email.description: The email address of the patient, used for communication and login.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the patient.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the patient.

addressStreet.description: The street address of the patient.

addressCity.description: The city or town of the patient's address.

addressCountry.description: The country of the patient's address.

allowLocationAccess.description: Indicates if the patient has allowed location access for services like nearby lab search or home collection.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the patient's profile image.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the patient registered on the platform.

LabPersonnel.title: LabPersonnel
LabPersonnel.description: Represents a certified laboratory personnel responsible for sample collection and result submission.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabPersonnel entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the lab personnel.

lastName.description: The last name of the lab personnel.

email.description: The email address of the lab personnel.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the lab personnel.

certifications.description: Details or URL referring to the professional certifications of the lab personnel.

isAvailableForHomeCollection.description: Indicates if the lab personnel is available for home sample collection.

assignedLabIds.description: References to Labs where this personnel is assigned or affiliated. (Relationship: LabPersonnel N:N Lab)

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the lab personnel registered on the platform.

LabOwner.title: LabOwner
LabOwner.description: Represents an owner of a laboratory who registers their lab on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabOwner entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the lab owner.

lastName.description: The last name of the lab owner.

email.description: The email address of the lab owner.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the lab owner.

ownedLabIds.description: References to Labs owned by this owner. (Relationship: LabOwner N:N Lab)

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the lab owner registered on the platform.

Lab.title: Lab
Lab.description: Represents a laboratory where tests can be processed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lab entity.

name.description: The official name of the laboratory.

addressStreet.description: The street address of the laboratory.

addressCity.description: The city or town of the laboratory's address.

addressCountry.description: The country of the laboratory's address.

contactEmail.description: The contact email for the laboratory.

contactPhone.description: The contact phone number for the laboratory.

labOwnerId.description: Reference to the LabOwner who owns this laboratory. (Relationship: LabOwner 1:N Lab)

availableTestIds.description: References to Tests that this laboratory can perform. (Relationship: Lab N:N Test)

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the lab registered on the platform.

Test.title: Test
Test.description: Represents a specific type of laboratory test available on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Test entity.

name.description: The name of the test (e.g., 'Complete Blood Count').

description.description: A brief description of what the test entails and what it screens for.

price.description: The standard price for this test.

availableLabIds.description: References to Labs that are capable of performing this specific test. (Relationship: Test N:N Lab)

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date and time when the test details were last updated.

Request.title: Request
Request.description: Represents a patient's request for a specific laboratory test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Request entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who made the request. (Relationship: Patient 1:N Request)

testId.description: Reference to the Test requested by the patient. (Relationship: Test 1:N Request)

symptoms.description: Patient-provided symptoms relevant to the test request.

collectionMethod.description: The chosen method for sample collection (e.g., 'home', 'lab_visit').

preferredCollectionTime.description: The patient's preferred date and time for sample collection.

preferredCollectionLocation.description: Specific instructions or address for sample collection if 'home' method is chosen.

chosenLabId.description: Reference to the Lab chosen by the patient for analysis or visit. (Relationship: Lab 1:N Request)

requestStatus.description: The current status of the request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Allocated', 'SampleCollected', 'InProgress', 'ResultsUploaded', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

allocatedPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel allocated to this request for sample collection. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N Request)

allocationDate.description: The date and time when a lab personnel was allocated to the request.

sampleCollectionVerifiedByPatient.description: Indicates if the patient has confirmed that the sample was collected.

sampleCollectionVerificationDate.description: The date and time when the patient verified sample collection.

paymentStatus.description: The payment status of the request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Failed').

paymentAmount.description: The total amount required for the test and associated services.

paymentReferenceId.description: A unique reference ID for the payment transaction (e.g., Mpesa transaction ID, unique account number used).

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was successfully processed.

supportingFileUrls.description: URLs to any supporting files (e.g., doctor's prescription, previous reports) uploaded with the request.

requestDate.description: The date and time when the request was initially submitted by the patient.

TestResult.title: TestResult
TestResult.description: Contains the results of a completed laboratory test for a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestResult entity.

requestId.description: Reference to the Request associated with these results. (Relationship: Request 1:N TestResult)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient for whom these results belong. (Relationship: Patient 1:N TestResult)

testId.description: Reference to the Test type for these results. (Relationship: Test 1:N TestResult)

resultDetailsUrl.description: URL to the detailed test result document, typically a PDF, available for viewing and download.

resultSummary.description: A brief summary of the test results.

analysisDate.description: The date and time when the test analysis was completed.

submittedByPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel who submitted these results. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N TestResult)

aiInsight.description: AI-generated insights or analysis based on the patient's test history and current results.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the test results were submitted to the platform.

PersonnelRating.title: PersonnelRating
PersonnelRating.description: Records a patient's rating and feedback for a lab personnel after a service is rendered.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PersonnelRating entity.

personnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel being rated. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N PersonnelRating)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who submitted the rating. (Relationship: Patient 1:N PersonnelRating)

requestId.description: Reference to the Request associated with this rating, providing context. (Relationship: Request 1:N PersonnelRating)

rating.description: The numerical rating given by the patient (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional textual feedback or comment from the patient.

ratingDate.description: The date and time when the rating was submitted.

AbuseReport.title: AbuseReport
AbuseReport.description: Records a report of misconduct or abuse against a lab personnel by a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AbuseReport entity.

reportedPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel against whom the report is made. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N AbuseReport)

reportingPatientId.description: Reference to the Patient who submitted the report. (Relationship: Patient 1:N AbuseReport)

requestId.description: Reference to the specific Request during which the alleged misconduct occurred, providing context. (Relationship: Request 1:N AbuseReport)

reason.description: A brief reason or category for the report (e.g., 'Professional Misconduct', 'Delayed Service').

reportDetails.description: Detailed description of the alleged misconduct.

reportDate.description: The date and time when the report was submitted.

reportStatus.description: The current status of the report (e.g., 'Pending', 'UnderReview', 'Resolved', 'Dismissed').

definition.description: Stores individual patient profiles. The document ID (`patientId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the patient. This structure ensures path-based ownership for patient-specific data access.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the patient, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores individual lab personnel profiles. The document ID (`personnelId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the personnel. This ensures path-based ownership for lab personnel-specific data.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the lab personnel, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores individual lab owner profiles. The document ID (`ownerId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the lab owner. This ensures path-based ownership for lab owner-specific data.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the lab owner, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores general information about laboratories. Documents include `labOwnerId` for authorization. This collection is publicly readable for patients to browse, but write access is restricted to the owning lab owner.

params.name: labId
params.description: The unique identifier for the laboratory.

definition.description: Stores details of available tests offered on the platform. This collection is publicly readable for all authenticated users to browse. Write access is restricted to an administrative role or specific authorized users.

params.name: testId
params.description: The unique identifier for the test type.

definition.description: Stores test requests made by patients. Each document includes denormalized `patientId` (matching the parent `{patientId}`), `allocatedPersonnelId`, and `chosenLabOwnerId` (derived from `chosenLabId`) to ensure Authorization Independence for patients, allocated personnel, and lab owners without hierarchical lookups in rules.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the patient who made the request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific test request.

definition.description: Stores detailed test results for a specific patient. Each document includes denormalized `patientId` (matching the parent `{patientId}`), `requestId`, `testId`, `submittedByPersonnelId`, and `relevantLabOwnerId` (derived from the lab that processed it) for Authorization Independence, allowing patients, submitting personnel, and lab owners to access results without `get()` calls in rules.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the patient for whom these results belong.

params.name: resultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the test result document.

definition.description: Stores patient ratings and feedback for lab personnel, contextualized within a specific request. Documents include denormalized `patientId`, `personnelId`, and `requestId` to ensure Authorization Independence for the patient who submitted the rating and the personnel being rated.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the request to which this rating is associated.

params.name: ratingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the personnel rating.

definition.description: Stores patient reports of misconduct against lab personnel, contextualized within a specific request. Documents include denormalized `reportingPatientId`, `reportedPersonnelId`, and `requestId` for Authorization Independence for the reporting patient, the reported personnel, and for administrative review.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the request to which this abuse report is associated.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the abuse report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for DigiLab Connect is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure robust security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, authorization context is extensively denormalized. Each document that requires access control by a user (Patient, Lab Personnel, Lab Owner) or a specific entity (Lab, Request) will explicitly contain the relevant `id` fields. For example:
*   `Request` documents will include `patientId`, `allocatedPersonnelId`, and `chosenLabOwnerId` (derived from `chosenLabId`). This allows patients to access their own requests, allocated personnel to access their assigned requests, and lab owners to view requests directed to their labs, all without cross-collection lookups in rules.
*   `TestResult` documents will include `patientId`, `submittedByPersonnelId`, and `relevantLabOwnerId` (derived from the lab that processed the request). This facilitates direct authorization for patients, submitting personnel, and lab owners.
*   `PersonnelRating` and `AbuseReport` documents will include `patientId` (or `reportingPatientId`) and `personnelId` (or `reportedPersonnelId`) to ensure that the patient who created the rating/report, and the personnel involved, can be authorized directly.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports Query-Agnostic Permissions (QAPs) primarily through **Structural Segregation** and **Denormalization**.
*   **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** Publicly browsable data, such as `labs` and `tests`, resides in root-level collections (`/labs`, `/tests`). These collections have broad read permissions for all authenticated users but tightly controlled write permissions (e.g., only `LabOwner` for their labs, or an admin for global tests). This prevents mixing public and private data in the same collection, simplifying rule logic. User-specific data is placed in subcollections (e.g., `/patients/{patientId}/requests`) or includes `ownerId` fields, allowing queries to be naturally restricted by the path or document data without relying on security rules to filter results. For instance, a patient querying `/patients/{patientId}/requests` can only access documents where `{patientId}` matches their `request.auth.uid` due to the path structure, or where `patientId` is explicitly denormalized in the document and checked by rules for root collections.
*   **Denormalization for QAPs:** By embedding `patientId`, `allocatedPersonnelId`, `chosenLabOwnerId`, etc., directly within `Request` and `TestResult` documents, roles can efficiently query for their relevant data. A Lab Personnel can query all `Request` documents where `allocatedPersonnelId == request.auth.uid`. Similarly, a Lab Owner can query `Request` documents where `chosenLabOwnerId == request.auth.uid`. These queries are efficient because the authorization field is indexed within the document, and the rules can directly verify `request.auth.uid` against this field without needing to filter on arbitrary document content, which Firestore rules are not designed for.

This design prioritizes explicit authorization logic within the data itself, enabling clear, concise, and highly performant Firestore security rules.
